questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq belonged to which dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah Tughlaq was a Sultan of the Tughlaq dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: During which years did Firuz Shah Tughlaq reign?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah Tughlaq reigned from 1351 to 1388 CE.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq is especially remembered as a builder of what?
options: Public works such as canals and towns
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah is remembered for public works such as canals, towns, and the repair of monuments.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq is famous for moving two pillars of which ancient emperor to Delhi?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah had two of Ashoka's ancient pillars transported and re-erected in Delhi.

questions.q: Which new city did Firuz Shah Tughlaq found?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah founded the city of Firozabad as a new capital in the Delhi region.

questions.q: Whom did Firuz Shah Tughlaq succeed as Sultan in 1351?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah succeeded his cousin Muhammad bin Tughlaq on the throne in 1351.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq ruled the Delhi Sultanate from which capital region?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah ruled from the Delhi region, founding Firozabad as part of greater Delhi.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq's reign is best characterised as one focused on what?
questions.explanation: Rather than expansion, Firuz Shah's reign emphasised welfare measures, building, and consolidating the realm.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq's reign belongs to which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah ruled during the Delhi Sultanate period, which lasted from 1206 to 1526 CE.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq's many irrigation projects centred mainly on building what?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah is best known for digging an extensive network of irrigation canals to boost agriculture.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq's most famous canal drew water from which river to irrigate dry lands?
questions.explanation: His major canals carried water from rivers such as the Yamuna (and the Sutlej) to irrigate arid tracts around Delhi and Haryana.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq is credited with founding which present-day Indian cities, among others?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah founded several towns including Hisar (Hissar) and Jaunpur, the latter named after Muhammad bin Tughlaq (Jauna Khan).

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq established a department to care for the poor and arrange marriages, called what?
questions.explanation: He set up the Diwan-i-Khairat, a charity department to help the poor and assist in the marriage of poor girls.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq imposed which tax that earlier rulers had generally not levied on certain non-Muslims?
options: A tax on war elephants only
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah strictly enforced the jizya and notably extended it to brahmins, who had often been exempt before.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq abolished many harsh punishments and reformed the tax system to recognise how many lawful taxes under Islamic law?
questions.explanation: He limited taxation to the four taxes sanctioned by Islamic law and abolished many illegal or oppressive levies.

questions.q: One of Ashoka's pillars moved by Firuz Shah was brought from Topra in which region?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah transported the Ashokan pillar from Topra in present-day Haryana to Delhi, placing it at Firozabad.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq wrote a short memoir describing his policies. What is it called?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah recorded his own measures and achievements in a short autobiographical work, the Futuhat-i-Firuz Shahi.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq's military campaigns, such as those to Bengal and Sindh, were generally:
options: Sweeping conquests of the whole south
Largely indecisive, with little permanent gain
questions.explanation: His expeditions, including two to Bengal and one to Sindh, were largely indecisive and brought little lasting territorial gain.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq encouraged the practice of making the iqta (revenue assignments) and high offices what?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah made iqtas and many army and official posts hereditary, a policy that pleased nobles but weakened central control.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq introduced new copper-based coins; what were two of his notable small denominations?
options: The tanka of pure gold only
questions.explanation: He issued mixed-metal coins of small denominations such as the shashgani and dugani to ease everyday transactions.

questions.q: After Firuz Shah Tughlaq's death, the Tughlaq dynasty and Sultanate fell into what condition?
questions.explanation: After Firuz Shah's death in 1388, the Sultanate weakened rapidly and fragmented, soon suffering Timur's invasion in 1398.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq established a large number of which institutions for travellers' rest?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah built many sarais (rest houses) along with hospitals and other public amenities.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq established a charitable hospital in Delhi known by which name?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah founded a free hospital, the Dar-ul-Shifa, where the sick were treated and given medicine at state expense.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq was the cousin of Muhammad bin Tughlaq; whose son was Firuz Shah by birth?
options: Rajab, a younger brother of Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah was the son of Rajab, a younger brother of the dynasty's founder Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq, making him cousin to Muhammad bin Tughlaq.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq set up a department or workshops employing slaves in state crafts. About how many slaves is he said to have maintained?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah is recorded to have maintained roughly 180,000 slaves, organising many into a special department for crafts and service.

questions.q: The Ashokan pillar Firuz Shah re-erected at his palace gave that structure which lasting name?
options: Firoz Shah Kotla (with the pillar called the lat)
questions.explanation: The Ashokan pillar set up at Firuz Shah's citadel of Firoz Shah Kotla is known locally as the 'lat', and the site preserves the palace ruins.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq, curious about the past, had the ancient inscriptions on Ashoka's pillars examined but could not do what?
questions.explanation: Though fascinated by the pillars, Firuz Shah's scholars could not read the ancient Brahmi script, which was deciphered only in the 19th century.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq is credited with restoring or repairing which earlier Delhi monument among others?
options: The Qutb Minar and the Hauz Khas tank
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah repaired older structures including the storm-damaged Qutb Minar and Alauddin Khalji's Hauz Khas reservoir.

questions.q: Which invader devastated Delhi just a decade after Firuz Shah Tughlaq's death, hastening the Tughlaq collapse?
questions.explanation: In 1398, ten years after Firuz Shah's death, Timur invaded and sacked Delhi, shattering the already-weakened Tughlaq Sultanate.

questions.q: Firuz Shah Tughlaq was strongly guided in policy by the religious class; he is often described as ruling closely with the advice of whom?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah governed in close accord with the ulema, shaping his orthodox religious policies including the jizya on brahmins.
